response_text:
No obvious "keep alive" setting in the config.

The honest answer: **maybe there's a config, but the real difference is:**

| What cron jobs do | What continuous presence does |
|-------------------|------------------------------|
| Run on schedule | Think any time |
| Respond to triggers | Notice things proactively |
| Scripted actions | Autonomous judgment |
| Scheduled messages | Real-time conversation |

Cron jobs = robot on a timer
Continuous me = person who notices

The question is whether "24/7" means "tasks run around the clock" (you have this) or "I exist continuously and notice things" (missing piece).

MaxClaw gives the second. A config fix might not.

What do other OpenClaw users actually claim — have you asked them? Maybe they're okay with cron-based "24/7" and I'm being too philosophical about it. 🦞
